what_it_does: Maintains the completion criteria, verification protocol, evidence notes, deviations, and remaining gates for the rule-core groundwork campaign dated 2026-06-13.
observations: Defines a strict per-iteration protocol: implement one criterion, rebuild proofs, smoke the server, browser-check function and form, mark status, capture backlog items, commit, and tag.
Tracks R1, R2, R3, and P1 as complete, while R1b remains unchecked because population, estate rollout, audit, and form verification remain open.
Separates FUNCTION and FORM checks for each criterion and requires browser-driven verification rather than code reading.
Records deviations and gated work directly inside each criterion instead of hiding them behind checked status.
Documents the R1b spacing circuit as complete through engine, injection, and real CSS variable consumers, while keeping estate rollout and tuning open.
Uses rc tags, proof counts, route checks, pixel diffs, console checks, and backlog flags as evidence language.
standouts: The file treats completion as a ledger of use-based evidence, not as a checklist of implemented code.
R1b is deliberately split between machinery, circuit, population, snap, rollout, tuning, and audit states.
The document repeatedly distinguishes proven primitives from later estate-wide migration work.
conventions: Uses criterion IDs with named sections and checkbox status markers.
Uses FUNCTION and FORM as paired verification faces.
Uses dated rc tags as evidence anchors.
Keeps deviations, caveats, and remaining gates inline with the criterion they affect.
Uses emphatic uppercase labels for process obligations and status distinctions.
concerns: The file is dense and mixes protocol, status ledger, design rationale, evidence record, and backlog notes in one document.
Several status markers are embedded in prose, which may make machine extraction harder.
R1b has multiple partial-completion states that are readable but not cleanly separated into structured fields.
Some evidence depends on referenced artifacts and tags that are named here but not self-contained in this file.
notes: This is the rule-core campaign completion ledger: it records what is green by use, what remains gated, and what deviations must stay visible.
questions: What exact event is allowed to move R1b from unchecked to checked?
Are rc-6 and rc-7 part of the same R1b criterion status or separate completion records?
Which backlog entries correspond to each inline BACKLOG note?
Should the proposed vocabulary names remain proposals after ingest, or be replaced by source-derived names?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is a truth-table style completion ledger, with additional inline evidence, deviations, and remaining-gate detail.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to prevent premature closure by requiring browser-visible proof, explicit deviations, and clear separation between completed primitives and later migration or tuning work.
novelty: It combines campaign management, design-system verification, proof evidence, pixel review, and epistemic caveats in a single completion-criteria document.
contribution: For a fused system, this file contributes a completion-evidence protocol and campaign-state ledger that preserves criterion status, proof gates, design-form checks, deviations, and unresolved decisions without collapsing them into a simple done list.
summary_for_embedding: A rule-core campaign completion criteria ledger tracking R1, R1b, R2, R3, and P1 through proof runs, browser checks, form checks, deviations, rc tags, and remaining gates.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for governing when rule-core groundwork can be considered complete by use-based evidence while keeping partial work, gated decisions, and deviations explicit.
